annotations.label: Hyperbola as conic section

anchor.snippet: If the plane intersects both halves of the double cone but does not pass through the apex of the cones, then the conic is a hyperbola.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of conic sections or cone-plane intersections defining a hyperbola.

annotations.label: Hyperbola as locus of points

anchor.snippet: A hyperbola can be defined geometrically as a set of points ( locus of points ) in the Euclidean plane:

annotations.note: No locus-of-points definition of a hyperbola exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Center of the hyperbola

anchor.snippet: of the line segment joining the foci is called the center of the hyperbola

annotations.note: No notion of a hyperbola's center is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Major axis

annotations.note: No hyperbola major-axis definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The 3-point-form equation of a hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: This equation is called the canonical form of a hyperbola

anchor.snippet: The axes of symmetry or principal axes are the transverse axis

anchor.snippet: the hyperbola is symmetric with respect to both of the coordinate axes

annotations.label: Asymptotes

anchor.snippet: These two lines intersect at the center (origin) and are called asymptotes of the hyperbola

annotations.label: Hyperbolic functions via unit hyperbola

anchor.snippet: so also the hyperbolic functions are defined in terms of the unit hyperbola

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Real.sinh/Real.cosh via exp rather than geometrically via the unit hyperbola.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic angle

anchor.snippet: The analogous hyperbolic angle is likewise defined as twice the area of a hyperbolic sector

annotations.note: The hyperbolic angle as twice the area of a hyperbolic sector is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflection (optical) property

anchor.snippet: This is called the optical property or reflection property of a hyperbola

annotations.note: The reflection/optical property of a hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Midpoints of parallel chords

anchor.snippet: The midpoints of parallel chords of a hyperbola lie on a line through the center

annotations.note: The parallel-chord midpoint (diameter) result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthoptic circle

anchor.snippet: the intersection points of orthogonal tangents lie on the circle

annotations.note: The orthoptic (director) circle of a hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pole-polar relation

annotations.note: Pole-polar relation with respect to a hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: For a point (pole) on the hyperbola the polar is the tangent at this point

annotations.label: Symmetry group is Klein four-group

anchor.snippet: the symmetry group of a hyperbola is the Klein four-group

annotations.note: The Klein four-group is formalized as IsKleinFour, but its identification with a hyperbola's symmetry group is not.

annotations.label: Squeeze mapping invariance

anchor.snippet: admit group actions by squeeze mappings which have the hyperbolas as invariant sets

annotations.note: Squeeze mappings and their hyperbola invariance are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Center as Poncelet point

anchor.snippet: The center of a rectangular hyperbola is the Poncelet point of any four points on it

annotations.note: The Poncelet point and this rectangular-hyperbola property are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arc length not elementary

anchor.snippet: The arc length of a hyperbola does not have an elementary expression

annotations.note: Non-elementarity of hyperbola arc length (Liouville-type result) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse curve is lemniscate of Bernoulli

anchor.snippet: the inverse curve is the lemniscate of Bernoulli

annotations.note: Neither the lemniscate of Bernoulli nor this inverse-curve result is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Confocal hyperbolas and elliptic coordinates

anchor.snippet: A family of confocal hyperbolas is the basis of the system of elliptic coordinates

annotations.note: Confocal hyperbolas and elliptic coordinates are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonality to confocal ellipses

anchor.snippet: Every hyperbola in this family is orthogonal to every ellipse that shares the same foci

annotations.note: Orthogonality of confocal hyperbolas and ellipses is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Image of a circle is a hyperbola

annotations.label: Sundial shadow traces a hyperbola

anchor.snippet: the shadow of the tip of a pole traces out a hyperbola on the ground over the course of a day

annotations.note: The sundial-shadow hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multilateration

anchor.snippet: A hyperbola is the basis for solving multilateration problems

annotations.note: Multilateration via hyperbolas is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unbound Kepler orbit is a hyperbola

anchor.snippet: the path of such a particle is a hyperbola

annotations.note: Hyperbolic Kepler orbits are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Korteweg–de Vries soliton solution

anchor.snippet: appears as one solution to the Korteweg–de Vries equation

annotations.note: The KdV equation and its soliton solution are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angle trisection

anchor.snippet: a hyperbola can be used to trisect any angle

annotations.note: Angle trisection via a hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The efficient portfolio frontier as a hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: They are both rectangular hyperbolae

annotations.note: The Hill/Michaelis–Menten rectangular hyperbola is not formalized in Mathlib.
